640020a88d3cf1e72e234523.question: A letter by this author expresses delight that Maria Congreve is reading “my demigod Milton.” This author emulated the style of Paradise Lost for a poetic drama in which Father Isidor demands that his nephew Silva choose between his love for Fedalma and his duty to the Catholic church. One of this author’s characters marries an older man because she idealizes him as Milton’s “affable archangel” who will help her “arrive at the core of things.” Anna Nardo’s book about this author’s “dialogue” with Milton discusses how Milton’s daughters inspired several of this author’s characters, including a girl who dutifully assists her blind father (*) Bardo de Bardi. This author of The Spanish Gypsy praised Milton’s tracts on divorce in a review which implicitly likened Milton to George Henry Lewes, who lived with her. For 10 points, name this Victorian author of Romola who depicted Edward Causabon’s miserable marriage to Dorothea Brooke in Middlemarch.

This author emulated the style of Paradise Lost for a poetic drama in which Father Isidor demands that his nephew Silva choose between his love for Fedalma and his duty to the Catholic church. One of this author's characters marries an older man because she idealizes him as Milton's "affable archangel" who will help her "arrive at the core of things." Anna Nardo's book about this author's "dialogue" with Milton discusses how Milton's daughters inspired several of this author's characters, including a girl who dutifully assists her blind father (*) Bardo de Bardi. This author of The Spanish Gypsy praised Milton's tracts on divorce in a review which implicitly likened Milton to George Henry Lewes, who lived with her. For 10 points, name this Victorian author of Romola who depicted Edward Causabon's miserable marriage to Dorothea Brooke in Middlemarch.

640020a88d3cf1e72e234526.question: This author lampooned Richard Bentley for altering Paradise Lost’s line “darkness visible” to “a transpicuous gloom” in his pamphlet Milton Restor’d and Bentley Deposed. Many of this author’s writings have been preserved because they were printed in George Faulkner’s edition of his collected works, as recounted in a landmark biography of this author by Irvin Ehrenpreis. A mock classical poem by this author is named for its two title characters, one an anagram of his profession and the other a reference to Esther Vanhomrigh. This author wrote “We have just religion enough to make us hate, but not enough to make us love one another” in his Thoughts on Various Subjects, Moral and Diverting, which also provided the title for A (*) Confederacy of Dunces. His Latin epitaph claims that his heart can no longer be torn by “sæva Indignatio,” or savage indignation. For 10 points, name this author of “Cadenus and Vanessa” more famous for writing A Meditation Upon a Broomstick and A Tale of a Tub.

His Latin epitaph claims that his heart can no longer be torn by "sæva Indignatio," or savage indignation. For 10 points, name this author of "Cadenus and Vanessa" more famous for writing A Meditation Upon a Broomstick and A Tale of a Tub.

640020a88d3cf1e72e234527.question: Jacob Tonson’s edition of Paradise Lost printed Milton’s portrait above this author’s “epigram,” which claims that Milton combined Homer’s “loftiness of thought” with Virgil’s “majesty.” This author argued that John Donne’s “amorous verses” fail because he “perplexes the minds of the fair sex with nice speculations of philosophy, when he should engage their hearts, and entertain them with the softnesses of love” in his Discourse Concerning the Original and Progress of Satire. This author’s enemies regularly embarrassed him by referencing youthful “heroical stanzas” he wrote commemorating the death of Oliver Cromwell. This author wrote The State of Innocence, a pretty bad stage version of Paradise Lost, which was published the same year he became (*) Poet Laureate. T. S. Eliot’s essay “The Metaphysical Poets” blames Milton and this other author for the 17th-century “dissociation of sensibility” which ruined English poetry. For 10 points, name this author who made up for his praise of Cromwell by praising the restoration of Charles II in his royalist poem Astraea Redux.

This author argued that John Donne's "amorous verses" fail because he "perplexes the minds of the fair sex with nice speculations of philosophy, when he should engage their hearts, and entertain them with the softnesses of love" in his Discourse Concerning the Original and Progress of Satire. This author's enemies regularly embarrassed him by referencing youthful "heroical stanzas" he wrote commemorating the death of Oliver Cromwell. For 10 points, name this author who made up for his praise of Cromwell by praising the restoration of Charles II in his royalist poem Astraea Redux.

640020a88d3cf1e72e23452e.question: Daniel Johnson and Beth Lau produced a digital version of this author’s annotated copy of Paradise Lost. In a letter, this author wrote “I have but lately stood on my guard against Milton. Life to him would be death to me.” This author compared himself to a votary offering “a burnt sacrifice of verse” to Milton’s “sacred and ennobled hearse” in a poem whose opening line salutes Milton as “Chief of organic numbers!” A character in one of this author’s poems attempts to console his audience by citing the “eternal law” that “first in beauty should be first in might”; he abandoned that poem because “there were too many Miltonic inversions in it.” This author gave his copy of Paradise Lost to his good friend Maria Dilke. After viewing a relic owned by (*) Leigh Hunt, this author wrote “Lines on Seeing a Lock of Milton’s Hair.” For 10 points, name this Romantic poet whose reading of Milton inspired him to write an unfinished epic poem about the fall of the Titans, Hyperion.
